body: Objective: Address reviewer's request-changes â€” fix the silent empty-response path (likely real root cause) and two other issues. Context: Reviewer approved your dedup/error-state work but found the actual "video don't load" cause is still open: when API returns 200 but no `embed_html` (empty data or missing field), component renders nothing with no feedback. Also `broadcast_status` param format may be returning empty results, and empty `videoLink` creates a self-referencing broken link. Reviewer findings (file:line refs are post-your-edits): **Critical fix 1 â€” `GetLiveVideo.tsx:26-28` silent empty response:** When `isCurrentlyLive` is false because `data: []` or no `embed_html`, `error` stays null and component renders blank. Add a "not live" fallback: when fetch completed, not loading, not live, no error â†’ render a message like "We're not live right now â€” check back soon." styled with brand orange #FD7B2B. You'll need a `hasChecked`/`hasFetched` state flag to distinguish "still loading" from "checked and not live." **Critical fix 2 â€” `route.ts:22` broadcast_status param:** Current: `url.searchParams.set("broadcast_status", '["LIVE"]')` â†’ encodes to `%5B%22LIVE%22%5D`. Facebook may not accept this format on all versions. Change to plain string `"LIVE"` (no JSON array): `url.searchParams.set("broadcast_status", "LIVE")`. If that's invalid for the endpoint, alternatively remove the filter entirely and filter client-side in GetLiveVideo.tsx by checking `first.status === "LIVE" || "LIVE_NOW"` (which you already do). Prefer removing the server filter + relying on client-side status check â€” simpler and avoids param-format ambiguity. **Critical fix 3 â€” `GetLiveVideo.tsx:88` broken link guard:** `` renders with empty string when `videoLink` is `""` â†’ self-referencing link. Guard it: only render the `` when `videoLink` is truthy. `{videoLink && (...)}`. Action Steps: 1. Read current `src/app/components/GetLiveVideo.tsx` and `src/app/api/live-video/route.ts`. 2. Apply fix 1: add `hasChecked` state, set true after fetch completes (success or fail), render "not live" fallback when `hasChecked && !isLive && !isLoading && !error`. 3. Apply fix 2: remove `broadcast_status` server filter in route.ts, rely on existing client-side `first.status` check. 4. Apply fix 3: guard the `` render on truthy `videoLink`. 5. Run `npm run lint` and `npx tsc --noEmit`. Constraints: - Preserve all behavior from your prior edits. - Keep brand orange #FD7B2B for fallback/error text. - Don't touch env var names or next.config.js. Deliverables: list of changes with line refs, lint/tsc results, confirm the three critical fixes are addressed.
